## Signature Format

Each row in the dashboard projects one record from `target/convergence/centicolon-signature.jsonl`. A signature record is the (metrics + timestamp + evidence-bundle hash) tuple defined by `methodology/proximity.yaml` and `methodology/litmus-centicolon-wiring.yaml`:

- `release_version` ‚Äî semver string identifying the release the snapshot belongs to.
- `release_date` ‚Äî UTC ISO 8601 timestamp of when the snapshot was produced.
- `source_commit` ‚Äî git sha that produced the snapshot.
- `expected_total_cc`, `actual_earned_cc`, `residual_cc` ‚Äî CentiColon budget, what the evidence earned, and what remains unclosed.
- `max_residual_spec`, `max_residual_reason` ‚Äî the spec id and reason carrying the largest remaining residual.
- `evidence_bundle_ref` ‚Äî path or hash of the evidence bundle (`target/convergence/evidence-bundle.json`) that backs the signature.
- `ci_result` ‚Äî PASS / FAIL of the CI run that produced the snapshot.

## Refresh Policy

The dashboard is regenerated from the signature log every time `update-convergence-dashboard.sh` runs (typically after a metrics-producing CI cycle).

- Local CI may regenerate after any metrics-producing run.
- Main-branch merges SHOULD append a new signature and refresh the dashboard.
- Release runs SHOULD publish dashboard + signature log + delta + evidence bundle together.
- The `generated_at` timestamp at the top of this file is the staleness signal. If it is older than 24 hours, downstream consumers SHOULD treat the snapshot as stale.

## Alert Thresholds

| Level | Trigger | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `red` | `percent_closed < 90%` | Implementation has drifted materially from spec; investigate immediately. |
| `yellow` | `percent_closed < 95%` | Emerging gap; review residual cc and worst-spec reason. |
| `green` | otherwise | Residual cc within acceptable bounds. |

## Integration & Interpretation

This dashboard is a **read-only projection** of the evidence semantics defined by `openspec/specs/knowledge-source-of-truth/spec.md`. The dashboard MUST NOT be hand-edited. To change what is reported:

- Change the upstream signature log (`target/convergence/centicolon-signature.jsonl`), or
- Change the renderer (`scripts/update-convergence-dashboard.sh`), or
- Change the methodology files (`methodology/proximity.yaml`, `methodology/litmus-centicolon-wiring.yaml`).

Related specs:

- `openspec/specs/knowledge-source-of-truth/spec.md` ‚Äî authority hierarchy, CRDT-inspired convergence, evidence bundles.
- `openspec/specs/observability-convergence/spec.md` ‚Äî coverage, latency, staleness, dashboard reporting requirements.
- `openspec/specs/spec-traceability/spec.md` ‚Äî `@trace spec:<name>` semantics that link signatures to code.
- `openspec/specs/methodology-accountability/spec.md` ‚Äî CentiColon residual proximity boundary.

Related cheatsheets:

- `docs/cheatsheets/centicolon-dashboard.md` ‚Äî visual contract, tail compression, anti-gaming rules.
- `cheatsheets/observability/cheatsheet-metrics.md` ‚Äî metric definitions and aggregation patterns.
- `cheatsheets/runtime/cheatsheet-crdt-overrides.md` ‚Äî CRDT discipline this dashboard inherits.
